research_question: Can public SEC QQQ N-PORT filings support a point-in-time top-50 disclosed-equity universe without reconstructing the full US security master?
null_hypothesis: The public filings cannot provide five stable cross-year snapshots with at least 50 uniquely identified long USD equity positions and distinct public availability timestamps.

scope.security_class_rule: Each filed security class is a distinct investable position; no issuer-level consolidation.
scope.equity_rule: assetCat=EC, payoffProfile=Long, curCd=USD, valUSD>0, non-empty CUSIP, non-empty issuer name and title.
scope.ranking_rule: Sort eligible positions by filed valUSD descending; CUSIP then title are deterministic tie breakers.
scope.availability_rule: Use SEC acceptanceDateTime from the registrant submissions feed. The report date is never an availability timestamp.
scope.ticker_rule: Ticker is optional in N-PORT smoke. CUSIP plus filed issuer/title is the required identity; ticker mapping is a later input gate.

fail_closed_rule: Any failed gate yields INPUT_BLOCKED. Do not replace report dates, relax security identity, infer availability from report dates, or access returns.
